description: Manual route-design holds for areas where graph coverage is known but the current outing is not a good human route.

areas.title: 16A Sweet Connie / Shingle / Sheep Camp manual design area
areas.decision: Do not schedule the Hawkins-start Sweet Connie + Shingle/Sheep placeholder as a normal outing. Keep Stack Rock Connector as a clean 16B outing, and redesign the remaining official segments from a lower Sweet/Dry access or a validated mixed-mode route.
covered_elsewhere_notes: 2026-06-19 repair: the prior 15A-1 Shingle ownership assumption is retracted because the active menu no longer has 15A-1 and the Shingle/Dry Creek field route must be repaired as a natural loop instead of a package ownership split.
Do not drop Shingle Creek 1656 from this design area unless a current regenerated route card owns it with human-valid Shingle-up / Dry-Creek-down field cues.

current_placeholder.label: 16A
current_placeholder.reason: Coverage placeholder with excessive access/return mileage from Hawkins; not recommended as a runnable outing.

anchors.name: Dry Creek / Sweet Connie roadside parking
notes: SWIMBA describes parking at the Dry Creek Trailhead on Bogus Basin Road across from Sweet Connie.
Local route writeups describe this as roadside parking / gravel pull-off parking, not an official full-service trailhead; verify signage day-of.
This is not a private home/work origin.

anchors.name: Lower Shingle / Dry Creek OSM parking west
notes: OSM Overpass returned amenity=parking within 2 km of the Shingle Creek lower endpoint on 2026-05-06.
Use current signage day-of; do not use private residential or closed road access.

anchors.name: Lower Shingle / Dry Creek OSM parking east
notes: OSM Overpass returned amenity=parking within 2 km of the Shingle Creek lower endpoint on 2026-05-06.
Use current signage day-of; do not use private residential or closed road access.

alternatives.title: Sweet Connie ascent from lower Sweet/Dry access
design_notes: Sweet Connie should be treated as the climb, not a Hawkins errand.
If the GPX cannot preserve uphill credit cleanly, split or redesign before scheduling.

alternatives.title: Sheep Camp lower loop
design_notes: Use the Sheep Camp-only lower loop only after Shingle Creek 1656 is owned by a current field-valid route card.
Do not rely on the retracted 15A-1 latent-credit promotion for Shingle ownership.

alternatives.title: Dry Creek / Shingle natural loop
design_notes: Field shape is lower Dry Creek access to the Shingle junction, Shingle Creek uphill for official ascent credit, then Dry Creek back down to the car.
This route replaces the planner artifact where 16C-2 climbed and descended Shingle while Dry Creek credit lived in 15B.
Lower Dry Creek repeat is expected from this parking anchor; Shingle repeat is not the normal field loop.

alternatives.title: All-section Sweet Connie + Stack + Shingle + Sheep experiment
design_notes: Reject as default if over 30 miles on foot or over 7 hours moving; 30-32 miles remains experimental only.

alternatives.title: Optional shuttle/drop-off comparison
design_notes: Allowed only as a separately-accounted variant.
Bike, car, or rideshare travel cannot count as on-foot challenge progress.

acceptance_gates: Sweet Connie official segments 1665, 1666, and 1667 traverse uphill.
Shingle Creek official segment 1656 traverses uphill.
No GPX point gap over 0.05 miles; prefer 0.03 miles or less.
Shingle Creek official segment 1656 is owned by a current field-valid route card before package 16 drops it from any selected alternative.
Dry Creek / Shingle natural-loop candidates climb Shingle and return down Dry Creek; package ownership must not force a Shingle out-and-back.
Replacement beats the current 36.48-mile Hawkins placeholder by at least 8 on-foot miles.
All-section route is rejected as default if over 30 on-foot miles or over 7 hours moving.
preflight_notes: Check current Ridge to Rivers/Bogus/Forest Service conditions before scheduling.
Freddy's Stack Rock remains a separate clean 16B candidate, but Stack Rock trailhead/gate status and potable water must be checked before use.

areas.title: Package 10 Harlow's / Spring Creek split probe
areas.decision: The selected Dry Creek-start all-in-one route pays a large connector/return tax between Currant Creek and Harlow's/Spring Creek. Test a split into a Dry Creek lower outing plus a Harlow/Hidden Springs-side outing.

current_placeholder.reason: Exact generated route is best within the existing candidate universe but still has 13.38 overhead miles.

anchors.name: Harlow's / Hidden Springs west access probe
notes: This is an access probe, not verified public parking.
Do not promote to field-ready until parking/signage/access is checked on the ground or against a current public map source.

anchors.name: Avimor Spring Valley Creek parking
notes: OSM Overpass returned amenity=parking, fee=no, capacity=36 near the Avimor/Spring Valley Creek trail network on 2026-05-06.
AllTrails Spring Valley Creek page reports free large-lot parking and recent easy-to-park user reports.
Use current signage day-of; do not use private residential parking.

anchors.name: Avimor Twisted Spring parking
notes: OSM Overpass returned amenity=parking, capacity=16 near the Avimor/Spring Valley Creek trail network on 2026-05-06.
Use current signage day-of; do not use private residential parking.

alternatives.title: Harlow's / Spring Creek from west-side access probe
design_notes: This should eliminate the long Currant Creek to Harlow's connector from the selected route.
Parking/access verification is the blocker, not graph continuity.

alternatives.title: Bitterbrush + Currant Creek from Dry Creek
design_notes: Keep the lower Dry Creek/Currant work near Dry Creek rather than dragging it into the Harlow's/Spring outing.

acceptance_gates: Combined 10A + 10B on-foot mileage improves on the current 23.14-mile selected route by at least 2 miles.
Both probes are graph-validated and have GPX continuity max gap under 0.05 miles.
Do not count any non-foot travel as challenge progress.
preflight_notes: Verify Harlow's / Hidden Springs west access and parking before using 10A in the field.

areas.title: Route-truth mismatch holds
areas.decision: Hold active route cards whose cue mileage and live-map/GPX span materially disagree. These are planner artifacts until repaired, not field insights.

current_placeholder.label: 11 route-source holds
current_placeholder.reason: Certification found cue/map mileage drift large enough to make the route source untrustworthy.

affected_routes.label: 1B
affected_routes.failure: cue 7 spans 1.32 GPX mi for 0.09 cue mi

affected_routes.label: 15B
affected_routes.failure: cue 3 spans 2.19 GPX mi for 1.42 cue mi

affected_routes.failure: Stack Rock Connector cue span disagrees with GPX
multiple Military Reserve cue spans disagree
Corrals connector cue span disagrees with GPX

affected_routes.failure: Sweet Connie cue span disagrees with GPX

multiple Three Bears / Freestone cue spans disagree

acceptance_gates: Do not publish these candidates as runnable field cards until cue mileage, live-map/GPX route spans, and canonical route topology reconcile.
Each repaired route must regenerate from canonical source and pass the field-packet certification gate without a blocked_navigation_source audit.
If a route cannot be repaired, keep its official segments in a manual design hold instead of exporting a planner artifact.
